definition.description: Pacific Coast Therapy Hub — Compliance Audit Flow. Performs 7-Layer XAI forensic audit of a clinical note. Authoritative sources: Microsoft Learn Power Platform, CMS Chapter 15, APTA Documentation Guidelines, AOTA Documentation Framework, ASHA Clinical Documentation Standards.

text.description: The full clinical note text to be audited
text.title: ClinicalNoteText

text_1.description: Discipline of the note (PT, OT, SLP)
text_1.title: Discipline

text_2.description: Document type (Progress Note, Eval, Recert, Discharge, Daily Note)
text_2.title: DocumentType

parameters.item/requestv2/DocumentationStandards:
@{concat('### PACIFIC COAST 7-LAYER XAI COMPLIANCE AUDIT ###

AUTHORITATIVE SOURCES:
- Microsoft Learn: Power Platform Healthcare AI Best Practices
- CMS Medicare Benefit Policy Manual Chapter 15
- APTA Documentation Guidelines (2023)
- AOTA Occupational Therapy Practice Framework (4th Ed.)
- ASHA Clinical Documentation Standards
- Jimmo v. Sebelius (2013) Maintenance Therapy Standard
- CMS MDS 3.0 RAI Manual

DISCIPLINE: ', triggerBody()?['text_1'], '
DOCUMENT TYPE: ', triggerBody()?['text_2'], '

NOTE TO AUDIT:
', triggerBody()?['text'], '

PERFORM 7-LAYER FORENSIC AUDIT:

LAYER 1 — SKILLED SERVICE JUSTIFICATION
Does the note contain a clear, measurable skilled justification per CMS Ch.15? Does it address the Jimmo maintenance standard if applicable? Does it specify why a non-licensed provider cannot safely deliver this care?

LAYER 2 — FUNCTIONAL BASELINE & PROGRESS
Are functional levels documented with specific assist levels (Min A, Mod A, Max A, etc.)? Is there a measurable comparison to prior status or PLOF? For PT: reference APTA Guide to Physical Therapist Practice. For OT: reference AOTA Occupational Profile. For SLP: reference ASHA functional communication/swallowing measures.

LAYER 3 — GOAL ALIGNMENT
Do the interventions documented align with the active goals? Are all active goals referenced? Are goals SMART and time-bound per APTA/AOTA/ASHA standards?

LAYER 4 — MEDICAL NECESSITY & COMPLEXITY
Are complicating factors documented that justify skilled oversight? Are clinical reasoning and judgment explicitly demonstrated?

LAYER 5 — SHAP/LIME LOGIC WEIGHTING
Assign confidence weights (0-1.0) for: Skilled Need (SHAP), Functional Measurement (SHAP), Goal Alignment (LIME), Medical Necessity (LIME), Plan Specificity (SHAP). Calculate composite risk score.

LAYER 6 — ADVERSARIAL FIREWALL
Scan for vague language triggers: "tolerated well", "participated", "cooperative", "patient did well". Flag each with specific replacement suggestions using discipline-specific clinical terminology.

LAYER 7 — OVERALL AUDIT VERDICT & REMEDIATION
Overall denial risk: LOW (0-30%), MEDIUM (31-60%), HIGH (61-85%), CRITICAL (86-100%). List top 3 specific remediations with exact language recommendations.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Structured audit report with each layer clearly labeled. Include composite risk score and final verdict.')}

auditresult.title: AuditResult
auditresult.description: 7-Layer XAI forensic audit report with risk score and remediation guidance
